name: e22-plugins
description: Element 22 plugin catalog — the AI-native collaborative workflow from vibes to production. Two lanes (Prototype, Production), one Product Spine, six house-rule plugins applied to every Claude session. For Claude Code and Claude Cowork.

plugins.name: prototype-lane
plugins.description: Prototype Lane plugin for Product Owners. /vibe spins up a sandboxed branch with a live preview within minutes. /package-handoff distills the prototype into a Product Spine and requests engineer validation. /proposal-status reports state without jargon. — auto-detects greenfield/brownfield projects before scaffolding.

plugins.name: production-lane
plugins.description: Production Lane plugin for engineers. /validate is the Keep/Refactor/Redesign/Reject gate. /propose, /from-design, /promote drive the production lifecycle. spec-refiner and drift-monitor agents keep specs honest.

plugins.name: spec-driven-dev
plugins.description: House rule: a spec or test must exist before code is generated. Lane-aware — lenient on prototype branches, strict on production-lane branches.

plugins.name: always-test
plugins.description: House rule: every new endpoint, screen, or background job ships with at least one smoke test. Lane-aware coverage requirements. — soft warning on prototype, hard block on production per spec §9.10.

plugins.name: house-style
plugins.description: House rule: naming, folder layout, and lint rules are the same in every PR. Runs the product's configured linter/formatter on edited files.

plugins.name: security-rails
plugins.description: House rule: blocks universal risky patterns by default (secrets, AWS access keys, Stripe live keys, raw-SQL string interpolation, force-push, push-to-main, prod database clients). Pairs with Anthropic's `security-guidance` for code-injection patterns.

plugins.name: spine-writer
plugins.description: House rule: the Product Spine stays in sync with the code. /spine-refresh and a PostToolUse hook keep the artefact that travels honest.

plugins.name: handoff-packager
plugins.description: House rule: every prototype-to-production handoff ships with a structured bundle (dependency delta, novel patterns, plugin violations) the engineer can validate in minutes.
